papers.title: Dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength FeeMneAleC steel
papers.paper_scope: Study hot deformation behavior, microstructure evolution and dynamic recrystallization of low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steel; establish DRX state diagram and grain size prediction model.

processes.description: Isothermal constant strain rate compression at temperatures 900–1150°C and strain rates 0.01–10 s^{-1}, with 60% reduction (true strain 0.92). Heating to 1200°C at 10°C/s, holding for 120 s, cooling to test temperature at 5°C/s, holding for 15 s, then compression and water quenching.
processes.processes_notes: Friction reduced by mica foils with high-temperature lubricants. Cylindrical samples, diameter 8 mm, height 12 mm.

structures.overall_structure: Partially recrystallized, fine DRX grains along grain boundaries.

phases_present.morphology: equiaxed DRX grains + deformed original grains

characterization_methods.purpose: Isothermal constant strain rate compression tests
characterization_methods.key_findings: Flow stress curves obtained, peak stress sensitive to temperature and strain rate; DRX activation energy calculated.
DRX grain size and morphology determined; etching with 30% nitric acid alcohol.

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Correlation coefficient R=0.996, mean relative error MRE=4.53%

analytical_model.model_name: DRX average grain size prediction model
analytical_model.model_note: Levenberg-Marquardt optimization used; friction factor 0.3, convection coefficient 0.02 N/(s·mm·°C)

analytical_model.model_name: Arrhenius hyperbolic sine equation for DRX
analytical_model.parameters: Q_p = 669.844 kJ/mol, α=0.00599, n=5.135 (average slope), etc.
analytical_model.model_note: Used to derive Zener-Hollomon parameter and characteristic strain/stress relationships.

unmapped_findings: The critical strain ratio εc/εp ≈ 0.452 was determined using the Cingara-McQueen method and Poliak-Jonas criterion.
Peak stress at 0.01 s^{-1} decreased from 305.4 MPa (900°C) to 53.8 MPa (1150°C), a reduction of 82%; at 10 s^{-1} the reduction was 73% over the same temperature range.
Z parameter values (lnZ) for all conditions are given in Table 1, e.g., at 900°C/0.01 s^{-1} lnZ=64.08, at 1150°C/10 s^{-1} lnZ=58.92.
Optimal hot working windows for fine and uniform grains: 950–1000°C/0.01–0.1 s^{-1} and 1050–1100°C/1–10 s^{-1}.
The initial microstructure (Fig. 1) showed austenite grains; after compression, deformation zones (Fig. 8) exhibited different DRX degrees (zone 3 > zone 2 > zone 1).
DRX grain size exceeded 70 μm at 1150°C/0.01 s^{-1}, indicating rapid grain growth.
Flow stress curves in Fig. 3 show typical DRX characteristics with stress peaks.
The steel is a negative temperature-sensitive and positive strain rate-sensitive material.
Reproducibility of experimental phenomena was confirmed through repeated tests on same batch.
